title: Genista ascendens
intro: Genista ascendens, also known as the upright broom or mountain broom, is a flowering shrub native to the Mediterranean region, belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Genista ascendens include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, such as genistein, kaempferol, and quercetin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Genista ascendens include teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as tinctures and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health issues and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Genista ascendens is classified within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume or pea family. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other species. Genista ascendens is further classified within the subfamily Faboideae and the tribe Genisteae. The genus Genista is a group of flowering plants that are native to Europe and Western Asia. Genista ascendens specifically is native to the mountainous regions of Europe.
